summary: Iteration 18 — End-to-end test of brand-new 'LeadNation Trade Command Center' feature on /customs-compliance plus Home page integration. Backend pytest 11/11 PASS (24s) — /api/command-center/quote returns ok:true with correct math (FOB=80500, CIF=85600, FOB perUnit=80.5, CIF perUnit=85.6) for HS 100630 India→Germany 1000 unit lane; 9-row waterfall with FOB Value + CIF Value milestone rows; destination block has dutyRate/vatRate/landed (Germany VAT=19% correctly applied); comparison array sorted ascending by buyerTotal with each row carrying code/country/cif/duty/vat; currency.converted populates fob/cif/landed/selling for global (USD) AND exporterLocal (INR); incentives array (RoDTEP/GST Refund/Duty Drawback/Advance Authorisation) returned for India origin 356; routes array carries Sea FCL + Sea LCL + Air. Validation: empty hs+product returns {ok:false, error:'Provide a product name or a 6-digit HS code.'}; product='rice' resolves via hs-search to a valid 6-digit HS. /api/command-center/markets returns 146+ countries sorted alphabetically by name. /api/command-center/insights returns ok:true with live:true and a 3159-char grounded markdown advisor referencing actual figures (INR 85600, INR 101864, INR 16264, Germany, United States, RoDTEP 1.0% of FOB). Frontend (Playwright): Home page has data-testid 'home-command-center' BEFORE 'home-services-highlight' with the exact tagline 'The World's First AI-Powered Global Trade Operating System.' and CTA 'home-command-center-cta' linking to /customs-compliance. /customs-compliance has 12 tabs, FIRST tab is customs-tab-compile labelled 'Trade Command Center' and is active by default. Live cc-fob-preview shows ₹80.50/unit and cc-cif-preview shows ₹85.60/unit as costs are typed in (re-renders correctly when transaction currency switches from $→₹→€). Clicking cc-submit (with HS 100630, India→Germany, qty=1000, costs filled, margin=15, INR/USD) triggers POST /api/command-center/quote (200) and renders cc-result with cc-kpis (FOB ₹80,500 ≈ $850.97 ≈ ₹80,500; CIF ₹85,600; Landed in Germany ₹101,864; Selling ₹98,440 — all three currencies on each KPI card), cc-waterfall (7 cost rows + FOB Value + CIF Value milestones), cc-destination (0% MFN duty, 19% VAT, landed ₹101,864), cc-comparison (8 markets, sorted ascending — top row 'United States' ₹85,600 has a green-highlighted top class + star icon), cc-currency (open.er-api.com source, 1 EUR=0.86 GBP shown on EUR retest), cc-incentives (RoDTEP 1.0% of FOB + GST Refund + Duty Drawback + Advance Authorisation), and cc-advisor — which after ~5s populates with a 3159-char 'LEADNATION BRAIN' markdown analysis grounded in the actual numbers/markets from the quote (cites Germany, United States, INR 85,600, INR 101,864, INR 16,264 VAT, RoDTEP 1.0% of FOB). Switching cc-txn-currency to EUR + cc-global-currency to GBP and re-running correctly updates KPI cards to €80,500.00 ≈ £69,389.55 ≈ ₹8,683,064.16 etc. Regression: customs-tab-report, customs-tab-duty, customs-tab-trade, customs-tab-fx all load their panels without error. NOTE on the failure I observed on attempt #1: I used the WRONG selector data-testid='cc-quantity' (the actual id is 'cc-qty'), so quantity stayed at the default 1 — this is a TEST script issue I corrected on attempt #2, not a product bug.
critical_code_review_comments: costing_engine.py:154-156 — globalCurrency normalises with .upper() but the request schema's default is 'USD'; if a client posts globalCurrency='' the .upper() yields '' and rates.get('') returns None so converted.global silently becomes null. Consider falling back to 'USD' when blank.

costing_engine.py:191 — comparison.sort puts the lowest buyerTotal first, but if dutyRate is None for ALL markets (rare WITS outage) every entry has buyerTotal == cif_t * (1 + vat/100) and sort is unstable across markets — UI then 'stars' an arbitrary one. Consider a tiebreaker by country name for deterministic UI.

costing_engine.py:194 — TRANSIT_DAYS only covers ~30 destinations; many countries return transit='varies' which is a fine fallback but the Routes block always shows three modes — consider hiding routes with no transit data or adding more entries.

costing_engine.py:230 — `sources` array lists 'World Bank WITS / UNCTAD TRAINS' even when wits_tariff returned None (cached miss). Consider trimming sources to those actually used in this quote so the advisor doesn't over-claim provenance.

costing_engine.py:282 — Brain question prompt is good and grounded, but engine_outputs.costing.data and market_comparison.data are passed as empty {} — the structured data could be richer (pass the full waterfall/comparison arrays so the LLM doesn't rely on the prose summary alone). Today the advisor is grounded because the prose summary is dense, but data-grounding is the safer pattern.

CustomsCompliance.jsx:511 — quantity input has data-testid='cc-qty' but the rest of the testids use full words (cc-search, cc-margin, cc-importer). Rename to 'cc-quantity' for consistency with the review request and downstream automation.

CustomsCompliance.jsx:524 — cc-margin and cc-qty inputs accept arbitrary text via type='number' but onChange stores e.target.value (string) — works because backend coerces, but negative or non-numeric input renders fine in UI and goes through; consider min='0' and parseFloat at the boundary.

CustomsCompliance.jsx:552 — cc-submit disables on loading but has no aria-busy / no inline spinner copy override; advisor 'Analysing…' state covers Brain only — the deterministic quote can take 10–15s on first uncached lane and the only feedback is the disabled state. Consider an explicit 'Building waterfall + comparing 8 markets…' message under the button.

CustomsCompliance.jsx:555 — cc-error shows generic 'Quote failed — please try again.' for any non-2xx; surface the backend's data.error string when available (the API already returns specific errors like 'Provide a product name or a 6-digit HS code.').

success_rate.frontend: 100% (home order+tagline+CTA, command-center quote end-to-end, all 7 result sections, currency switch, 4 regression tabs)

seed_data_creation: None (read-only quote engine, results cached to db.cc_cache but no user data written).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run backend: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_command_center.py -v` — 11 tests, ~24s; the insights test depends on Brain (gpt-5.4-mini) being live so allow 90s and rerun once if a transient brain timeout occurs. CRITICAL selector gotcha: quantity input is data-testid='cc-qty' NOT 'cc-quantity' as the review-request body suggests. To trigger a fresh uncached lane (and see the 10-15s first-load), pick a HS×origin×destination combo NOT in db.cc_cache. KPI cards show 3 currencies stacked vertically — assert presence of either the symbol (₹/$/€/£) OR the ISO code in the cc-kpis text; selling card is 4-currency: transaction (large) + global (≈) + exporterLocal (≈) + profit (in transaction). Home command-center section confirmed BEFORE services-highlight via compareDocumentPosition. Print/Save quote (PDF) and 'Ask the Brain' buttons exist (cc-print-btn, cc-ask-brain) but not exercised in this run.
